profile.name: Jake Barber
roles: U.S. Air Force Special Operations Veteran
Government Contractor - Covert UAP Retrieval Program
organizations: Unidentified Private Defense Contractor (UAP Retrieval Program)
profile.clearance: Top Secret / SCI (reported; exact compartments not publicly specified)
profile.summary: Jake Barber is a U.S. Air Force special operations veteran who came forward publicly in January 2025, in an exclusive interview with journalist Ross Coulthart for NewsNation, to disclose his firsthand participation in a covert government UAP crash retrieval program. Barber claims to have directly participated in multiple retrieval operations - including one involving an egg-shaped craft - and presented never-before-seen video footage of such an operation to NewsNation. Three additional high-level military veterans corroborated his account on camera. The Pentagon confirmed it was investigating his claims following broadcast. Barber is one of the first insiders to speak on the record with this level of operational specificity since David Grusch's 2023 congressional testimony.
education: U.S. Air Force training - specific details not publicly disclosed
early_career: Served in U.S. Air Force Special Operations Command
Transitioned to government contracting in classified programs
Claims to have been recruited into a compartmented UAP retrieval program during contractor tenure

key_events.event: Active duty U.S. Air Force, Special Operations. Specific units and roles not publicly disclosed.
Transitions to government contractor role; becomes involved with a compartmented UAP retrieval program - exact timeline not publicly specified.
Barber contacts Ross Coulthart of NewsNation to share his account. Corroboration gathered from three additional named military veterans.
Barber provides video footage and full on-camera account to NewsNation. The special 'Hunting UFOs: The Crash Retrieval Whistleblower' airs January 18, 2025 at 8 p.m. ET.
Pentagon confirms it has opened an investigation into Barber's claims following the NewsNation broadcast.

claims.claim: Barber directly participated in a covert U.S. government UAP crash retrieval program. He was part of a specialized team tasked with locating, securing, and recovering anomalous craft at crash or landing sites.
claims.notes: Barber provides firsthand operational details and on-camera testimony. Three additional military veterans corroborate the program's existence on camera. No government agency has confirmed or denied the program itself; Pentagon stated only that it was investigating.

claims.claim: During one retrieval operation, Barber's team recovered an egg-shaped, white craft of unknown origin. He describes the object as distinctly anomalous - not matching any known U.S. or foreign aerospace design.
claims.notes: Barber described the object as 'a white egg.' Video footage of the retrieval operation, described as never-before-seen, was presented to NewsNation and aired during the January 18, 2025 special.

claims.claim: Barber provided NewsNation with video footage of a UAP retrieval operation - described as the first time such operational footage has ever been publicly aired.
claims.notes: NewsNation broadcast the footage and reported it as genuine. Independent authentication of the footage's provenance and content has not been completed as of the broadcast date. The footage was edited/partially obscured to protect operational security and identities.

claims.claim: Three additional high-level military veterans with firsthand knowledge of the retrieval program appeared on camera to corroborate Barber's account.
claims.notes: NewsNation identified these individuals as high-level military veterans. Their testimonies were broadcast January 18, 2025. Individual names and service records were not all publicly identified in the initial broadcast.

claims.claim: Barber participated in multiple retrieval operations over the course of his involvement with the program, not a single isolated incident.
claims.notes: Stated in interview. No independent corroboration for the total number or scope of operations beyond the egg-shaped object incident presented on air.

claims.claim: Following the NewsNation broadcast, the Pentagon acknowledged it had opened an investigation into Barber's claims.
claims.notes: NewsNation reported the Pentagon's investigation confirmation following the broadcast. This represents a more direct governmental acknowledgment than was received for many earlier disclosures.

supporting: Three additional military veterans corroborated his account on camera - a higher level of contemporaneous on-record corroboration than most prior disclosures
Video footage of a retrieval operation was provided and broadcast - unprecedented in the modern UAP disclosure landscape
Pentagon's confirmation of an investigation following broadcast suggests the claims were taken seriously at an institutional level
Ross Coulthart - the journalist who broke the story - is one of the most credentialed UAP investigative reporters in the field and spent considerable time vetting the account prior to broadcast
NewsNation's editorial process and legal review would have required a high bar for airing such specific operational claims
Barber's Air Force special operations background is consistent with the type of personnel who would be recruited for compartmented recovery operations
Disclosure made voluntarily, with apparent personal risk, without financial incentive from the interview itself
contradicting: No government agency has confirmed the existence of the retrieval program Barber describes; the Pentagon's investigation is of his claims, not a confirmation
The video footage aired by NewsNation has not been independently authenticated by a third party with verified chain of custody
Barber's specific background, units served, and operational timeline are largely not publicly verifiable
Barber is less embedded in the established DC UAP disclosure community than figures like Grusch or Elizondo - raising questions about his institutional positioning
No formal ICIG complaint or legal whistleblower filing has been publicly confirmed, unlike Grusch's 2022 protected disclosure
The specific corroborating witnesses were not all fully identified in the initial broadcast, limiting independent verification

associated_people.name: Ross Coulthart
associated_people.role: Investigative journalist, NewsNation; author of In Plain Sight (2021)
associated_people.relationship: Conducted the primary interview with Barber; spent significant time vetting the account and corroborating witnesses before broadcast. The longest-serving UAP investigative journalist in the modern era.

associated_people.name: David Grusch
associated_people.role: Former NRO representative, DoD UAP Task Force
associated_people.relationship: Grusch's 2023 testimony - the first to describe crash retrieval programs at a congressional level - preceded and contextualized Barber's disclosure. Barber's claims are operationally complementary to Grusch's institutional account.

disclosures.title: Hunting UFOs: The Crash Retrieval Whistleblower
disclosures.notes: First public disclosure. Barber appeared on camera with operational details, retrieval video footage, and four on-record military veterans total. Pentagon confirmed investigation following broadcast.

Barber's January 2025 disclosure followed the pattern established by Grusch's 2023 congressional testimony on crash retrieval programs
